description_long: This monastery is a large, rectangular brick and stone structure with four prominent corner towers, each topped with steep, pointed roofs. The walls are adorned with a mix of red brick and white plaster, featuring decorative patterns and small windows, some of which are arched. The main building encloses a spacious open courtyard, typical of monastic complexes, and the roofs are covered with reddish-brown tiles. The towers display intricate brickwork and white accents, with one tower distinguished by a red roof. The overall design suggests a fortified religious complex, blending defensive and residential elements. The architecture is reminiscent of Renaissance-era Eastern European monasteries, with a harmonious balance of symmetry and ornamentation.
description: A fortified monastery with four tall corner towers, red brick walls, and a central open courtyard, featuring Renaissance architectural details.
description_view_0: Front view showing two ornate brick towers flanking the main entrance and a clear view into the central courtyard.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long, tiled roof and the red-roofed tower at the left corner, with fewer visible windows.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the longest wall with minimal ornamentation and two towers at each end.
description_view_3: Opposite front view with two towers and a clear perspective into the courtyard, emphasizing the symmetry of the structure.
